chief_complaint: A 41-year-old man presents with recurrent angina.
history: The patient had undergone percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) and initially showed improvement, but angina recurred after one month due to refusal of stent implantation. He has a history of subtotal occlusion of the first diagonal branch (D1).
exam: No specific physical examination findings are mentioned.
labs: Magnetocardiography (MCG) initially suggested myocardial ischemia, which returned to normal after revascularization. A second MCG showed myocardial ischemia again with a normal electrocardiogram (ECG) and cardiac troponin I (cTNI). A second coronary angiography (CAG) confirmed D1 restenosis.
